item.title: Football will eclipse politics at this World Cup
item.description: Goals, talent and diversity are set to drown out controversy off the pitch

item.title: SpaceX’s IPO is a display of Musk’s autocratic power
item.description: His unrivalled grip on the public imagination has transmuted into Wall Street gold

item.title: European defence stocks retreat on lower spending and higher wrangling
item.description: Resignation of UK defence secretary shows some governments will struggle to find the money

item.title: Trump’s bill threatens the singleness of money
item.description: It might be improper to put the president’s face on the currency. It might also not work

item.title: Sooner or later, America is going to have to reckon with the debt
item.description: There is a bigger political constituency for fiscal consolidation than many think

item.title: The Palantir controversy is a block on NHS progress
item.description: Better patient outcomes are at risk in the backlash against American tech

item.title: SpaceX is cheap on a price-to-cosmos ratio
item.description: Terrestrial valuations don’t apply when it comes to Elon

item.title: Elon Musk is a real-life Bond villain
item.description: The X owner is using his platform to amplify extremist opponents of multiculturalism in Britain

item.title: Why do employers think it’s OK to ghost job applicants?
item.description: Businesses that complain it is hard to recruit the right staff should look at themselves too

item.title: The joke that deepened Starmer and Burnham’s toxic rift
item.description: Relations between the politicians — both suspicious towards each other — sank to new lows after PM’s mocking comment

item.title: The ECB tightens course
item.description: Plus debt restructurings and de-dollarisation

item.title: Musk should be bolder with his fantastical projections
item.description: Financial history offers examples of now-giant companies that formerly lowballed their own prospects

item.title: The great bond and equity conundrum
item.description: Financial logic has been stood on its head as yields buck decades-long trends

item.title: Japan’s PM: Building resilience in a turbulent world
item.description: To tackle the challenges of today, it is essential to acquire new capabilities

item.title: Kate Moss, mermaids and climbing icebergs: glorious roses to plant
item.description: Those that thrive in hot climates, others that flower monthly, or which have unusual hues. Take your pick, but choose wisely

item.title: The AI public market floodgates are opening
item.description: The huge sums that Wall Street is about to be asked for look like being only a down payment

item.title: The Iran war is testing the limits of India’s economic resilience
item.description: Modi will need to combine emergency measures with reforms to help the country rebound

item.title: New York Knicks’ winning streak should rub off on its owner too
item.description: Shares in Madison Square Garden Sports, which also owns hockey team the New York Rangers, have doubled in a year

item.title: SpaceX IPO marks the end of the no-brainer benchmark index trade
item.description: Investors do not need big benchmarks to get what they want

item.title: The ‘new joule order’ is here. The west is last to realise
item.description: Electrification is the purchase of optionality and China bought more of it than any nation in history

item.title: The unlikely alliance pushing an AI sovereign wealth fund
item.description: Even some of the tech labs seem to agree that society as a whole should benefit from advances

item.title: Why Europe should not choose to go it alone
item.description: A secure future depends on a revitalised transatlantic alliance

item.title: The rise of the pro-worker conservative is changing the US
item.description: Republicans helping to strengthen labour law would once have seemed an outlandish prospect — no longer

item.title: The business model of white victimhood
item.description: Rage on the right and a narrative of disadvantage changing sides is rewarded by social media

item.title: How bond markets can learn to love public debt
item.description: There is ample room to be expansive within market-friendly fiscal rules
